message:
fix(acfs): propagate explicit TARGET_HOME through resolve chain + harden shell-embedded paths

This is a system-wide hardening pass on ACFS's target-home resolution
and on the way library scripts embed filesystem paths into child bash
processes. It addresses two distinct but related classes of bug found
while running ACFS against hosts where the invoking user's $HOME does
not match the TARGET_USER's passwd entry (e.g. root-invoked installs
for an `ubuntu` target, or sudo-preserved environments where HOME
leaks across identity boundaries).

================================================================
1) _acfs_resolve_target_home now takes an explicit expected_home arg
================================================================

`_acfs_resolve_target_home` previously had one source of truth: NSS
(getent passwd) and, as a fallback, the current process's $HOME when
the current user matched TARGET_USER. The fallback was unconditional,
so a caller that had *already* decided on an explicit TARGET_HOME (via
env, wrapper script, or --target-home flag) would silently get a
different path back if $HOME happened to disagree with TARGET_HOME.

Every resolver on the path now:
  - Accepts an optional explicit_home parameter
  - Normalizes it (absolute, no trailing slash, never "/")
  - Short-circuits to it when the $HOME-based fallback would otherwise
    produce a conflicting value
  - Returns only values that agree with the explicit hint when one
    was given

Callsite updates across the codebase now pipe the explicit value in:
  - install.sh (`acfs_summary_emit`, `generate_resume_hint`, setup
    flow): passes `explicit_target_home` through to the resolver and
    restores it as the fallback when resolution fails
  - scripts/lib/install_helpers.sh: inline `_acfs_resolve_target_home`
    grows the `expected_home` parameter; `run_as_target` now probes
    the passwd DB first and only uses the resolver with the explicit
    hint if passwd lookup misses
  - scripts/lib/update.sh (`update_target_home`): gates the
    HOME-matches-current-user shortcut on explicit_home agreement
  - scripts/lib/doctor.sh + scripts/generated/doctor_checks.sh: the
    generated tool-resolution helper captures `explicit_target_home`
    up front, normalizes it, and feeds it into the resolver
  - scripts/generated/install_*.sh (all 15 per-module install scripts
    plus doctor_checks.sh, install_all.sh, internal_checksums.sh,
    manifest_index.sh): regenerated from
    `packages/manifest/src/generate.ts` which now emits the explicit-
    home shape in the shared module prelude
  - scripts/lib/{agents,autofix,changelog,cli_tools,cloud_db,context,
    continue,dashboard,export-config,github_api,info,languages,
    os_detect,report,security,session,stack,status,support,user}.sh
    and scripts/services-setup.sh, scripts/acfs-global,
    scripts/acfs-update: updated to the new resolver signature and
    made explicit about passing through the caller's requested
    TARGET_HOME rather than blindly re-resolving
  - acfs.manifest.yaml: the AGENTS.md placement fragment and the
    shared resolve prelude regenerate to match

================================================================
2) Eliminate path-data injection into bash -c subshells
================================================================

`_run_shell_with_strict_mode` in scripts/lib/install_helpers.sh used
to splice `$ACFS_BIN_DIR`-derived paths directly into a `bash -c`
argument:
    bash -c "$env_setup; set -euo pipefail; $cmd"
That is an unsafe pattern when path components can contain shell
metacharacters. Rewritten to:
    env ACFS_TARGET_PATH_PREFIX=... UV_NO_CONFIG=1 \
        bash -c 'export PATH="${ACFS_TARGET_PATH_PREFIX}:$PATH"; ...' _ "$cmd"
The path data now lives in the environment, not the shell source, and
the user command is passed as a positional so no command substitution
occurs during expansion. UV_NO_CONFIG is preserved (prevents uv from
picking up /root config during sudo).

================================================================
3) cleanup_legacy_git_safety_guard: structured jq walk, not grep
================================================================

scripts/lib/update.sh's migration cleanup previously decided whether
to scrub `git_safety_guard` entries from `~/.claude/settings.json`
using `grep -q git_safety_guard`. That matched any stringified
occurrence, including unrelated comments or settings that merely
reference the name. It also had different `jq` walker semantics
depending on whether `settings.json` used the object-keyed or
array-shaped `hooks` schema.

Replaced with `update_claude_settings_has_legacy_git_safety_guard_hook`,
a pure-jq predicate that:
  - Only matches `.type == "command"` hook entries whose `.command`
    string actually contains `git_safety_guard`
  - Walks both hook schema shapes (object with event keys -> array of
    entries, or top-level array) via explicit `hook_event_entries`
    + `event_entry_matches` defs
  - Returns a clean boolean so callers don't have to juggle jq exit
    codes against grep exit codes

The cleanup path now invokes the predicate both in the dry-run
detection and in the actual-edit branch, dropping the grep fallback
entirely.

================================================================
4) Tests
================================================================

  - tests/unit/lib/test_update.bats: +1434 lines covering the new
    `update_claude_settings_has_legacy_git_safety_guard_hook`
    predicate across both schema shapes and
    `update_target_home`/explicit-home semantics
  - tests/unit/lib/test_install_helpers.bats: exercises the env-based
    path prefix plumbing and the explicit-home signature of
    `_acfs_resolve_target_home` / `run_as_target`
  - tests/unit/lib/test_security.bats, test_session.bats,
    test_user.bats: updated to pass explicit expected_home where the
    production code now requires it
  - tests/unit/test_github_api.sh, test_resume_hint.sh: coverage for
    the install.sh resume-hint changes (quoted URL, array-based
    argument accumulation with --resume, --mode, --ref, and --skip-*)
  - packages/manifest/src/generate.test.ts: regression coverage for
    the manifest generator's new prelude shape and for the updated
    `doctor_checks.sh` emission
  - packages/onboard/src/lib/authChecks.ts +
    __tests__/authChecks.test.ts: onboard's auth-check helpers now
    share the explicit_target_home semantics used by the installer

================================================================
5) install.sh: safer resume-hint construction
================================================================

`generate_resume_hint` previously built the resume command by string
concatenation, which interpolated `$install_url` and user-visible
flag values directly. Rewritten to:
  - `printf -v install_url_q '%q' "$install_url"` so the URL is shell-
    safely quoted into the `curl ... | bash -s --` form
  - Accumulate flags in a bash array `resume_args=(--resume ...)` and
    `%q` each one when emitting, avoiding accidental word-splitting
    and making it trivial to add new skip-flags without breaking
    quoting

================================================================
6) Beads bookkeeping
================================================================

`.beads/issues.jsonl` picks up the two new open tracking beads noted
in the earlier commit (bd-0iiu5 for leftover .tsconfig artifacts and
bd-elkig for the Bun+Turbopack SIGILL watch), plus normal progress
on existing entries from other agents. No closures reversed.

No code paths were removed; every diff is additive hardening or an
in-place refactor of an existing helper to accept the explicit-home
argument.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-0ge","title":"[ubu.4] Implement upgrade execution logic","description":"# Implement Upgrade Execution Logic

## Purpose
Core logic for executing do-release-upgrade non-interactively with proper error handling.

## Key Functions

### ubuntu_prepare_upgrade()
Prepares system for upgrade:
```bash
# 1. Update package lists
apt-get update

# 2. Install any pending upgrades
apt-get dist-upgrade -y

# 3. Install update-manager-core if missing
apt-get install -y update-manager-core

# 4. Clean up old packages
apt-get autoremove -y
apt-get clean
```

### ubuntu_do_upgrade()
Performs the actual upgrade:
```bash
# Run do-release-upgrade non-interactively
# -f DistUpgradeViewNonInteractive: No prompts
# -m server: Use server release channels
DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive \
  do-release-upgrade \
    -f DistUpgradeViewNonInteractive \
    -m server \
    2>&1 | tee -a "$UPGRADE_LOG"
```

### ubuntu_trigger_reboot()
Handles reboot with state preservation:
```bash
# Update state to indicate reboot needed
state_upgrade_needs_reboot

# Sync filesystem
sync

# Delay to ensure state is written
sleep 2

# Reboot
systemctl reboot
```

## Non-Interactive Mode Details

do-release-upgrade has several ways to run non-interactively:

1. **-f DistUpgradeViewNonInteractive**: Uses dpkg defaults for all prompts
2. **DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive**: Tells dpkg to not prompt
3. **-m server**: Uses server upgrade channel (more stable than desktop)

## Error Handling

### Common Failure Modes
1. **Disk full**: Caught by preflight, but also check exit code
2. **Package conflicts**: Log details, suggest manual resolution
3. **Network failure**: Retry with exponential backoff
4. **Interrupted upgrade**: Can often recover with dpkg --configure -a

### Recovery Actions
```bash
ubuntu_recover_failed_upgrade() {
  log_warn "Attempting to recover from failed upgrade..."
  
  # Fix any interrupted dpkg operations
  dpkg --configure -a
  
  # Fix broken dependencies
  apt-get -f install -y
  
  # Try the upgrade again
  ubuntu_do_upgrade
}
```

## Logging

All output goes to /var/log/acfs/upgrade.log:
- Timestamped entries
- Full apt output for debugging
- Clear phase markers
- Error details

## Estimated Runtime

Per upgrade hop:
- apt update/upgrade: 5-10 minutes
- do-release-upgrade: 20-45 minutes
- Reboot: 2-5 minutes
- Total per hop: ~30-60 minutes

For 24.04 → 25.10 (3 hops): 1.5-3 hours total","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T21:21:19.431419Z","updated_at":"2025-12-21T21:40:38.660185Z","closed_at":"2025-12-21T21:40:38.660185Z","close_reason":"Core upgrade execution logic already implemented: ubuntu_prepare_upgrade (apt update, dist-upgrade, clean), ubuntu_do_upgrade (do-release-upgrade with DistUpgradeViewNonInteractive), ubuntu_trigger_reboot (graceful shutdown with delay).  {"id":"bd-0iq","title":"Create scripts/preflight.sh validation script","description":"# Task: Create scripts/preflight.sh validation script

## Purpose
Validate system prerequisites before installation begins to fail fast with clear errors.

## Checks to Implement

### 1. System Requirements
```bash
check_os() {
    [[ -f /etc/os-release ]] || fail "Not a Linux system"
    source /etc/os-release
    [[ "$ID" == "ubuntu" ]] || warn "Untested OS: $ID (Ubuntu recommended)"
    
    # Version check
    local version="${VERSION_ID:-0}"
    (( ${version%%.*} >= 22 )) || warn "Ubuntu 22.04+ recommended"
}

check_architecture() {
    local arch=$(uname -m)
    [[ "$arch" == "x86_64" || "$arch" == "aarch64" ]] || \
        fail "Unsupported architecture: $arch"
}

check_memory() {
    local mem_gb=$(awk "/MemTotal/ {printf \"%.0f\", \$2/1024/1024}" /proc/meminfo)
    (( mem_gb >= 4 )) || warn "Low memory: ${mem_gb}GB (8GB+ recommended)"
}

check_disk() {
    local free_gb=$(df -BG / | awk "NR==2 {print \$4}" | tr -d "G")
    (( free_gb >= 20 )) || fail "Insufficient disk: ${free_gb}GB free (need 20GB+)"
}
```

### 2. Network Connectivity (Critical Addition)
```bash
check_network() {
    # Test basic connectivity
    curl -sf --max-time 5 https://github.com > /dev/null || \
        fail "Cannot reach github.com - check network/firewall"
    
    # Test key installer URLs
    for url in "https://bun.sh" "https://astral.sh" "https://claude.ai"; do
        curl -sf --max-time 5 "$url" > /dev/null || \
            warn "Cannot reach $url"
    done
}
```

### 3. APT Lock Check (Critical Addition)
```bash
check_apt_lock() {
    # Check for active apt processes
    if fuser /var/lib/dpkg/lock-frontend &>/dev/null; then
        fail "APT is locked by another process. Wait or run: sudo killall apt apt-get"
    fi
    
    # Check for unattended-upgrades
    if pgrep -x unattended-upgr &>/dev/null; then
        warn "unattended-upgrades running - may cause apt conflicts"
        warn "Consider: sudo systemctl stop unattended-upgrades"
    fi
}
```

### 4. User Environment
```bash
check_user() {
    [[ "$EUID" -ne 0 ]] || warn "Running as root not recommended"
    [[ -n "$HOME" ]] || fail "HOME not set"
    [[ -w "$HOME" ]] || fail "HOME not writable"
}

check_shell() {
    local shell=$(basename "$SHELL")
    [[ "$shell" == "bash" || "$shell" == "zsh" ]] || \
        warn "Untested shell: $shell"
}
```

### 5. Existing Conflicts
```bash
check_conflicts() {
    # Check for nvm (conflicts with mise)
    [[ \! -d "$HOME/.nvm" ]] || warn "nvm detected - may conflict with mise"
    
    # Check for pyenv (conflicts with uv)
    [[ \! -d "$HOME/.pyenv" ]] || warn "pyenv detected - may conflict with uv"
    
    # Check for existing mise
    command -v mise &>/dev/null && warn "mise already installed"
}
```

## Output Format
```
ACFS Pre-Flight Check
=====================
[✓] Operating System: Ubuntu 24.04
[✓] Architecture: x86_64
[✓] Memory: 16GB
[✓] Disk Space: 45GB free
[✓] Network: github.com reachable
[✓] APT: No locks detected
[\!] Warning: nvm detected - may conflict with mise
[✗] Error: Cannot reach claude.ai

Result: 1 error, 1 warning
```

## Exit Codes
- 0: All critical checks pass (warnings OK)
- 1: Critical check failed (installation would fail)

## Dependencies
- None (standalone script)
- Used by: install.sh integration (agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-545)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All system checks implemented
- [ ] APT lock detection added
- [ ] Network connectivity tests added
- [ ] Clear pass/warn/fail output
- [ ] Non-zero exit on critical failure
- [ ] Can run standalone: ./scripts/preflight.sh","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T17:43:13.693214Z","updated_at":"2025-12-21T19:41:41.971739Z","closed_at":"2025-12-21T19:41:41.971739Z","close_reason":"Created scripts/preflight.sh with all required checks: OS, arch, memory, disk, network, APT locks, user env, conflicts","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-eh383","title":"meta_skill ARM64 Linux binary missing — blocked upstream (GH #115)","description":"## Context
GitHub issue #115 filed by tayiorbeii (2026-02-06). On ARM64 Linux (aarch64), meta_skill install 404s because v0.1.0 has no aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu binary.

## Available binaries in v0.1.0:
- ms-0.1.0-aarch64-apple-darwin.tar.gz (ARM64 macOS - exists)
- ms-0.1.0-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu.tar.gz (x86_64 Linux - exists)
- ms-0.1.0-x86_64-pc-windows-msvc.zip (Windows - exists)
- aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu — MISSING

## Two-Part Fix

### Part 1: ACFS workaround (THIS repo, can do now)
Update doctor.sh check at lines 1115-1116 to detect aarch64 on Linux and show:
  "meta_skill: ARM64 Linux binary not yet available (see https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/meta_skill/issues/1)"
instead of the generic install suggestion that will 404.

Implementation:
```bash
# In doctor.sh, before the meta_skill install suggestion:
if [[ "$(uname -m)" == "aarch64" ]] && [[ "$(uname -s)" == "Linux" ]]; then
    log_item "warn" "meta_skill (ms)" "ARM64 Linux binary not yet available"
    return 0  # Skip install attempt
fi
```

### Part 2: Upstream (meta_skill repo, blocked)
Add aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu to the GoReleaser or release CI matrix in Dicklesworthstone/meta_skill. Requires:
- Cross-compilation target or dedicated ARM64 runner
- New release (v0.1.1 or v0.2.0)
This is blocked on upstream release work.

## Testing
After Part 1:
1. On this VPS (x86_64): `acfs doctor` should still show meta_skill normally
2. Simulate ARM64: mock uname output or grep the code to verify the aarch64 check exists
3. Verify the issue URL is correct and accessible

## Key Files
- scripts/lib/doctor.sh (lines 1109-1117, meta_skill check)
- Upstream: Dicklesworthstone/meta_skill release CI matrix

## Status
Part 1 is actionable now. Part 2 is blocked upstream. Keep this bead open until both parts are done.","notes":"ACFS-side mitigation is complete: Linux ARM64 now warns correctly in doctor.sh, builds meta_skill from source in the generated installer, scripts/lib/update.sh, and scripts/lib/doctor_fix.sh, and now has regression coverage in tests/unit/test_doctor_generated.sh plus manifest and doctor-fix/update tests. Remaining work is upstream binary publishing for aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu in meta_skill.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-08T18:16:39.015874050Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T00:54:56Z","closed_at":"2026-03-13T00:54:56Z","close_reason":"Closed as locally complete: ACFS now avoids the broken ARM64 Linux installer path and has regression coverage; remaining work belongs upstream in Dicklesworthstone/meta_skill issue #1.","external_ref":"gh:Dicklesworthstone/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup#115","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["arm64","bug","meta_skill","upstream-blocked"],"comments":[{"id":83,"issue_id":"bd-eh383","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Part 1 complete: doctor.sh updated to detect ARM64 Linux and show a helpful warning + build-from-source suggestion instead of the install script that would 404. Part 2 (upstream meta_skill binary publish) still pending.","created_at":"2026-02-11T17:11:27Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-eli","title":"Research CASS session data format and API","description":"# Task: Research CASS session data format and API

## Context
Part of EPIC: Agent Session Sharing and Replay (agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-0sb)

## Why This Comes First
Before we can export sessions, we need to understand how CASS stores them:
- What format is the session data?
- What commands does CASS expose?
- What fields are available for export?
- Are there multiple session types (Claude vs Codex vs Gemini)?

## Research Questions
1. Run 'cass --help' and document available commands
2. Run 'cass list' and examine output format
3. Run 'cass get <session_id> --json' and examine structure
4. Check if CASS stores sessions per-agent or unified
5. Identify what data is NOT stored (we can't export what doesn't exist)

## Deliverables
- Document CASS CLI commands and their output
- Example session data structure
- List of exportable fields
- Any limitations or gaps discovered

## Acceptance Criteria
- CASS API documented
- Session format understood
- Export schema can be designed based on actual data
- Gaps identified for follow-up

## Notes
This is research, not implementation. Output is documentation that informs subsequent tasks.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T17:51:13.705596Z","updated_at":"2025-12-21T20:52:01.467990Z","closed_at":"2025-12-21T20:52:01.467990Z","close_reason":"Research complete.  {"id":"bd-eooz","title":"Add password clarification (VPS root vs provider account)","description":"# Task: Add Password Clarification

## Parent Epic
[EPIC] First SSH Connection Experience (agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-q7r3)

## Description
Users frequently confuse their VPS provider account password (Hetzner/Contabo login) with the VPS root password (set during VPS creation). This is the #1 support issue. They try the wrong password, get locked out, and panic.

## Implementation Details
Location: apps/web/app/wizard/ssh-connect/page.tsx

Add a PROMINENT AlertCard above the SSH command:

\`\`\`tsx
<AlertCard variant="warning" icon={KeyRound} title="Which password to use">
  <div className="space-y-2">
    <p>
      When SSH asks for a password, use the <strong>VPS root password</strong> — 
      the one you created (or received via email) when setting up the VPS.
    </p>
    <div className="grid gap-2 sm:grid-cols-2 text-sm mt-3">
      <div className="rounded-lg border border-destructive/30 bg-destructive/10 p-3">
        <p className="font-medium text-destructive">❌ NOT this password:</p>
        <p className="text-muted-foreground">Your Hetzner/Contabo/OVH account login</p>
      </div>
      <div className="rounded-lg border border-[oklch(0.72_0.19_145/0.3)] bg-[oklch(0.72_0.19_145/0.1)] p-3">
        <p className="font-medium text-[oklch(0.72_0.19_145)]">✓ USE this password:</p>
        <p className="text-muted-foreground">The root password from VPS creation</p>
      </div>
    </div>
    <p className="text-sm text-muted-foreground mt-2">
      Some providers email you the root password. Check your inbox if you didn't set one.
    </p>
  </div>
</AlertCard>
\`\`\`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Appears PROMINENTLY, not buried in SimplerGuide
- [ ] Clear visual distinction between wrong and right password
- [ ] Mentions email option for some providers
- [ ] Uses warning styling to grab attention
- [ ] Works well on mobile (stacked layout)

## UI/UX Notes
- This is SO common that it deserves main-content placement
- The two-column comparison makes it instantly clear
- Mobile should stack vertically
- Consider adding link back to VPS creation step if they forgot","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T18:41:38.986261Z","updated_at":"2025-12-22T19:02:19.783445Z","closed_at":"2025-12-22T19:02:19.783445Z","close_reason":"Already covered: ssh-connect/page.tsx has AlertCard + Step 6 explaining VPS password vs hidden typing","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-epc","title":"[codex.1] Fix doctor.sh codex auth detection","description":"## Goal
Update scripts/lib/doctor.sh to correctly detect codex-cli authentication via OAuth tokens instead of checking for OPENAI_API_KEY.

## Current (Incorrect) Behavior
The check_codex_auth() function currently:
1. Checks for OPENAI_API_KEY environment variable
2. Looks for OPENAI_API_KEY in ~/.zshrc.local
3. Looks for OPENAI_API_KEY in /data/projects/.envrc
4. Reports "pass" if any of these exist

## Correct Behavior
Should instead:
1. Check for ~/.codex/auth.json (or similar OAuth token file)
2. Optionally verify the token isn't expired
3. Report "pass" if OAuth credentials exist
4. Suggest running "codex auth" if not authenticated

## Technical Notes
- Need to verify the actual path where codex-cli stores OAuth tokens
- The token file location may vary by OS/version
- Common locations: ~/.codex/, ~/.config/codex/, ~/.local/share/codex/

## Files to Modify
- scripts/lib/doctor.sh: Update check_codex_auth() function

## Acceptance Criteria
1. No mention of OPENAI_API_KEY in codex auth check
2. Correctly detects OAuth authentication
3. Provides helpful guidance for unauthenticated users
4. Regenerate scripts/generated/doctor_checks.sh after changes","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T21:25:35.325519Z","updated_at":"2025-12-21T21:37:13.931458Z","closed_at":"2025-12-21T21:37:13.931458Z","close_reason":"Fixed check_codex_auth to detect OAuth tokens in ~/.codex/auth.json instead of checking OPENAI_API_KEY env var.  {"id":"bd-eq0f","title":"Add dcg to required_tools array in install.sh","description":"## Task: Add dcg to required_tools array in install.sh

### What to Do

Add "dcg" to the required_tools array that validates checksums are available for all tools we install.

### Location

File: /data/projects/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup/install.sh
Line: ~1612

### Current Code

```bash
local required_tools=(
    atuin bun bv caam cass claude cm mcp_agent_mail ntm ohmyzsh rust slb ubs uv zoxide
)
```

### New Code

```bash
local required_tools=(
    atuin bun bv caam cass claude cm dcg mcp_agent_mail ntm ohmyzsh rust slb ubs uv zoxide
)
```

### Rationale

The required_tools array is checked during installer initialization to ensure we have checksums for all tools we plan to install. Without dcg in this list, the installer won't validate that dcg's checksum exists in checksums.yaml, which could lead to silent installation failures.

### Note

dcg is already in checksums.yaml (added previously), so this just enables validation.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-11T03:46:18.044341192Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T06:07:51.874177770Z","closed_at":"2026-01-11T06:07:51.874177770Z","close_reason":"Added dcg to required_tools array for upstream checksum validation","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-eq0f","depends_on_id":"bd-z7om","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T03:52:51.348683379Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}

+_agent_is_placeholder_secret() {
+    local normalized=""
+    normalized="$(_agent_normalize_config_value "${1-}")"
+    normalized="${normalized,,}"
+
+    case "$normalized" in
+        your-token-here|your_token_here|your-token|your_token|your_api_key|your-api-key|your_github_token|your_openai_api_key|your_claude_token|your_vercel_token|your_supabase_access_token|your_cloudflare_api_token|your_gemini_api_key|your_google_api_key|your_project_id|your_project_location|replace-me|change-me|changeme|"<token>"|"<api-key>"|"<secret>")
+            return 0
+            ;;
+    esac
+
+    return 1
+}
+
+_agent_has_usable_secret() {
+    local normalized=""
+    normalized="$(_agent_normalize_config_value "${1-}")"
+    [[ -n "${normalized//[[:space:]]/}" ]] && ! _agent_is_placeholder_secret "$normalized"
+}
+
+_agent_json_file_has_usable_jq_value() {
+    local file_path="${1:-}"
+    local jq_expr="${2:-}"
+    local candidate=""
+
+    [[ -s "$file_path" ]] || return 1
+    [[ -n "$jq_expr" ]] || return 1
+    command -v jq >/dev/null 2>&1 || return 1
+
+    while IFS= read -r candidate; do
+        if _agent_has_usable_secret "$candidate"; then
+            return 0
+        fi
+    done < <(jq -r "$jq_expr" "$file_path" 2>/dev/null || true)
+
+    return 1
+}
+
+_agent_json_file_has_usable_string_key() {
+    local file_path="${1:-}"
+    shift || true
+
+    [[ -s "$file_path" ]] || return 1
+
+    local key=""
+    local regex=""
+    local line=""
+    for key in "$@"; do
+        [[ -n "$key" ]] || continue
+        regex="\"${key}\"[[:space:]]*:[[:space:]]*\"([^\"]+)\""
+        while IFS= read -r line || [[ -n "$line" ]]; do
+            if [[ "$line" =~ $regex ]] && _agent_has_usable_secret "${BASH_REMATCH[1]}"; then
+                return 0
+            fi
+        done < "$file_path"
+    done
+
+    return 1
+}

+_acfs_doctor_read_json_string_key() {
+    local json_file="${1:-}"
+    local key="${2:-}"
+    local value=""
+    local jq_bin=""
+    local sed_bin=""
+
+    [[ -f "$json_file" ]] || return 1
+    [[ "$key" =~ ^[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*$ ]] || return 1
+
+    jq_bin="$(_acfs_doctor_system_binary_path jq 2>/dev/null || true)"
+    if [[ -n "$jq_bin" ]]; then
+        value="$("$jq_bin" -r --arg key "$key" 'select(.[$key] | type == "string") | .[$key]' "$json_file" 2>/dev/null || true)"
+    fi
+
+    if [[ -z "$value" ]]; then
+        sed_bin="$(_acfs_doctor_system_binary_path sed 2>/dev/null || true)"
+        if [[ -n "$sed_bin" ]]; then
+            value="$("$sed_bin" -n "s/.*\"${key}\"[[:space:]]*:[[:space:]]*\"\([^\"]*\)\".*/\1/p" "$json_file" 2>/dev/null | head -n 1)"
+        fi
+    fi
+
+    [[ -n "$value" ]] && [[ "$value" != "null" ]] || return 1
+    printf '%s\n' "$value"
+}
+
+_acfs_doctor_normalize_mode() {
+    local mode="${1:-}"
+
+    case "$mode" in
+        vibe|safe)
+            printf '%s\n' "$mode"
+            ;;
+        *)
+            return 1
+            ;;
+    esac
+}
+
+_acfs_doctor_normalize_ref() {
+    local ref="${1:-}"
+
+    [[ -n "$ref" ]] || return 1
+    [[ "$ref" =~ ^[A-Za-z0-9._/-]+$ ]] || return 1
+    [[ "$ref" != /* ]] || return 1
+    [[ "$ref" != */ ]] || return 1
+    [[ "$ref" != *..* ]] || return 1
+    [[ "$ref" != *@\{* ]] || return 1
+    printf '%s\n' "$ref"
+}

+_acfs_doctor_shell_has_active_assignment() {
+    local file="${1:-}"
+    local variable_name="${2:-}"
+    [[ -n "$file" && -n "$variable_name" && -f "$file" ]] || return 1
+
+    awk -v variable_name="$variable_name" '
+        /^[[:space:]]*#/ { next }
+        {
+            line=$0
+            sub(/^[[:space:]]*export[[:space:]]+/, "", line)
+            if (line ~ "^[[:space:]]*" variable_name "[[:space:]]*=") {
+                sub("^[[:space:]]*" variable_name "[[:space:]]*=", "", line)
+                sub(/[[:space:]]+#.*$/, "", line)
+                gsub(/^[[:space:]]+|[[:space:]]+$/, "", line)
+                empty_single_quotes=sprintf("%c%c", 39, 39)
+                if (line != "" && line != "\"\"" && line != empty_single_quotes) {
+                    found=1
+                    exit
+                }
+            }
+        }
+        END { exit(found ? 0 : 1) }
+    ' "$file" 2>/dev/null
+}
+
+_acfs_doctor_claude_settings_has_command_hook() {
+    local settings_file="${1:-}"
+    local command_pattern="${2:-}"
+
+    [[ -n "$settings_file" && -n "$command_pattern" ]] || return 1
+    [[ -f "$settings_file" ]] || return 1
+    command -v jq >/dev/null 2>&1 || return 1
+
+    jq -e --arg pattern "$command_pattern" '
+      def command_hook_matches:
+        type == "object"
+        and ((.type? // "command") == "command")
+        and ((.command? // "") | strings | test($pattern));
+      def event_entry_matches:
+        if type == "object" and (.hooks? | type) == "array" then
+          any(.hooks[]?; command_hook_matches)
+        else
+          command_hook_matches
+        end;
+      def hook_event_entries:
+        if (.hooks? | type) == "object" then
+          .hooks | to_entries[]? | .value | arrays | .[]?
+        elif (.hooks? | type) == "array" then
+          .hooks[]?
+        else
+          empty
+        end;
+      any(hook_event_entries; event_entry_matches)
+    ' "$settings_file" >/dev/null 2>&1
+}

+json_file_has_usable_jq_value() {
+    local file_path="${1:-}"
+    local jq_expr="${2:-}"
+    local candidate=""
+
+    [[ -s "$file_path" ]] || return 1
+    [[ -n "$jq_expr" ]] || return 1

+
+    while IFS= read -r candidate; do
+        if has_usable_secret "$candidate"; then
+            return 0
+        fi

+
+    return 1

+json_file_has_usable_string_key() {
+    local file_path="${1:-}"
+    shift || true
+
+    [[ -s "$file_path" ]] || return 1
+
+    local key=""
+    local regex=""
+    local line=""
+    for key in "$@"; do
+        [[ -n "$key" ]] || continue
+        regex="\"${key}\"[[:space:]]*:[[:space:]]*\"([^\"]+)\""
+        while IFS= read -r line || [[ -n "$line" ]]; do
+            if [[ "$line" =~ $regex ]] && has_usable_secret "${BASH_REMATCH[1]}"; then
+                return 0
+            fi
+        done < "$file_path"
+    done
+
+    return 1
+}

+_stack_claude_settings_has_command_hook() {
+    local settings_file="${1:-}"
+    local command_pattern="${2:-}"
+
+    [[ -n "$settings_file" && -n "$command_pattern" ]] || return 1
+    [[ -f "$settings_file" ]] || return 1
+    command -v jq >/dev/null 2>&1 || return 1
+
+    jq -e --arg pattern "$command_pattern" '
+      def command_hook_matches:
+        type == "object"
+        and ((.type? // "command") == "command")
+        and ((.command? // "") | strings | test($pattern));
+      def event_entry_matches:
+        if type == "object" and (.hooks? | type) == "array" then
+          any(.hooks[]?; command_hook_matches)
+        else
+          command_hook_matches
+        end;
+      def hook_event_entries:
+        if (.hooks? | type) == "object" then
+          .hooks | to_entries[]? | .value | arrays | .[]?
+        elif (.hooks? | type) == "array" then
+          .hooks[]?
+        else
+          empty
+        end;
+      any(hook_event_entries; event_entry_matches)
+    ' "$settings_file" >/dev/null 2>&1
+}

+@test "cleanup_legacy_git_safety_guard: removes only legacy command hook entries" {
+    local target_home

+

+
+    export HOME="$target_home"
+    export TARGET_HOME="$target_home"

+
+    mkdir -p "$(dirname "$settings_file")"

+{
+  "notes": "git_safety_guard was replaced by DCG",
+  "hooks": {
+    "PreToolUse": [
+      {
+        "matcher": "Bash",
+        "description": "old git_safety_guard docs, not a command",
+        "hooks": [
+          {
+            "type": "command",
+            "command": "dcg guard --source claude"
+          },
+          {
+            "type": "command",
+            "command": "/home/ubuntu/.claude/hooks/git_safety_guard.sh"
+          }
+        ]
+      }
+    ]
+  }
+}
+EOF
+
+    run cleanup_legacy_git_safety_guard
+    assert_success
+
+    run jq -r '.notes' "$settings_file"
+    assert_success
+    assert_output "git_safety_guard was replaced by DCG"
+
+    run jq -r '.hooks.PreToolUse[0].description' "$settings_file"
+    assert_success
+    assert_output "old git_safety_guard docs, not a command"
+
+    run jq -r '.hooks.PreToolUse[0].hooks | length' "$settings_file"
+    assert_success
+    assert_output "1"
+
+    run jq -r '.hooks.PreToolUse[0].hooks[0].command' "$settings_file"
+    assert_success
+    assert_output "dcg guard --source claude"
+}

+@test "services-setup: init_target_context ignores explicit other-user TARGET_HOME" {
+    local services_setup="$PROJECT_ROOT/scripts/services-setup.sh"
+    local target_home
+    local stale_home
+    local caller_home
+    local stale_bun
+    local target_bun
+    target_home="$(create_temp_dir)"
+    stale_home="$(create_temp_dir)"
+    caller_home="$(create_temp_dir)"
+    stale_bun="$stale_home/.local/bin/bun"
+    target_bun="$target_home/.local/bin/bun"
+
+    mkdir -p "$target_home/.local/bin" "$target_home/.acfs" "$stale_home/.local/bin" "$stale_home/.acfs"
+    touch "$stale_bun" "$target_bun"
+    chmod +x "$stale_bun" "$target_bun"
+
+    eval "$(sed -n '/^services_setup_sanitize_abs_nonroot_path()/,/^}$/p' "$services_setup")"
+    eval "$(sed -n '/^services_setup_valid_target_user()/,/^}$/p' "$services_setup")"
+    eval "$(sed -n '/^services_setup_validate_target_user()/,/^}$/p' "$services_setup")"
+    eval "$(sed -n '/^services_setup_passwd_home_from_entry()/,/^}$/p' "$services_setup")"
+    eval "$(sed -n '/^resolve_home_dir()/,/^}$/p' "$services_setup")"
+    eval "$(sed -n '/^services_setup_validate_bin_dir_for_home()/,/^}$/p' "$services_setup")"
+    eval "$(sed -n '/^find_user_bin()/,/^}$/p' "$services_setup")"
+    eval "$(sed -n '/^init_target_context()/,/^}$/p' "$services_setup")"
+
+    log_error() {
+        printf '%s\n' "$*" >&2
+    }
+
+    services_setup_getent_passwd_entry() {
+        if [[ -z "${1:-}" ]]; then
+            printf 'acfstestuser:x:1000:1000::%s:/bin/bash\n' "$target_home"
+            printf 'stale-user:x:1001:1001::%s:/bin/bash\n' "$stale_home"
+            return 0
+        fi
+        if [[ "${1:-}" == "acfstestuser" ]]; then
+            printf 'acfstestuser:x:1000:1000::%s:/bin/bash\n' "$target_home"
+            return 0
+        fi
+        return 1
+    }
+
+    export TARGET_USER="acfstestuser"
+    export TARGET_HOME="$stale_home"
+    export HOME="$caller_home"
+    export ACFS_BIN_DIR="$stale_home/.local/bin"
+    export ACFS_HOME="$stale_home/.acfs"
+    export BUN_BIN="$stale_bun"
+    _SERVICES_SETUP_ENV_HOME="$caller_home"
+
+    init_target_context
+
+    [[ "$TARGET_HOME" == "$target_home" ]] || {
+        printf 'TARGET_HOME was not repaired: %s\n' "$TARGET_HOME" >&2
+        return 1
+    }
+    [[ "$ACFS_BIN_DIR" != "$stale_home/.local/bin" ]] || {
+        printf 'ACFS_BIN_DIR still points at stale home\n' >&2
+        return 1
+    }
+    [[ "$ACFS_HOME" == "$target_home/.acfs" ]] || {
+        printf 'ACFS_HOME was not repaired: %s\n' "$ACFS_HOME" >&2
+        return 1
+    }
+    [[ "$BUN_BIN" == "$target_bun" ]] || {
+        printf 'BUN_BIN was not repaired: %s\n' "$BUN_BIN" >&2
+        return 1
+    }
+}

+@test "global wrappers repair stale ACFS env before cross-user re-exec" {
+    local update_wrapper="$PROJECT_ROOT/scripts/acfs-update"
+    local global_wrapper="$PROJECT_ROOT/scripts/acfs-global"
+    local wrapper=""
+    local function_body=""
+    local target_home
+    local stale_home
+    local fake_sudo
+    local label
+
+    for wrapper in "$update_wrapper" "$global_wrapper"; do
+        label="${wrapper##*/}"
+        target_home="$(create_temp_dir)"
+        stale_home="$(create_temp_dir)"
+        fake_sudo="$BATS_TEST_TMPDIR/$label-sudo"
+
+        mkdir -p "$target_home/.acfs" "$stale_home/.acfs"
+        cat > "$target_home/.acfs/state.json" <<EOF
+{
+  "target_user": "acfstestuser",
+  "target_home": "$target_home"
+}
+EOF
+        cat > "$stale_home/.acfs/state.json" <<EOF
+{
+  "target_user": "staleuser",
+  "target_home": "$stale_home"
+}
+EOF
+        cat > "$fake_sudo" <<'EOF'
+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+printf '%s\n' "$@"
+EOF
+        chmod +x "$fake_sudo"
+
+        if [[ "$wrapper" == "$update_wrapper" ]]; then
+            function_body="$(sed -n '/^exec_as_target_user()/,/^}/p' "$wrapper")"
+        else
+            function_body="$(sed -n '/^    exec_as_target_user()/,/^    }/p' "$wrapper")"
+        fi
+
+        run env -i \
+            PATH="/usr/bin:/bin" \
+            TARGET_HOME="$target_home" \
+            ACFS_HOME="$stale_home/.acfs" \
+            ACFS_STATE_FILE="$stale_home/.acfs/state.json" \
+            ACFS_SYSTEM_STATE_FILE="$stale_home/.acfs/state.json" \
+            TEST_FAKE_SUDO="$fake_sudo" \
+            TEST_FUNCTION_BODY="$function_body" \
+            bash -c '
+                set -euo pipefail
+                eval "$TEST_FUNCTION_BODY"
+                sanitize_abs_nonroot_path() {
+                    local path_value="${1:-}"
+                    [[ -n "$path_value" ]] || return 1
+                    path_value="${path_value%/}"
+                    [[ -n "$path_value" ]] || return 1
+                    [[ "$path_value" == /* ]] || return 1
+                    [[ "$path_value" != "/" ]] || return 1
+                    printf "%s\n" "$path_value"
+                }
+                is_valid_username() {
+                    [[ "${1:-}" =~ ^[a-z_][a-z0-9._-]*$ ]]
+                }
+                validate_target_user_or_die() {
+                    is_valid_username "${1:-}" || exit 77
+                }
+                state_file_matches_target_home() {
+                    [[ "${1:-}" == "$TARGET_HOME/.acfs/state.json" ]] && [[ "${2:-}" == "$TARGET_HOME" ]]
+                }
+                system_binary_path() {
+                    case "${1:-}" in
+                        env) printf "/usr/bin/env\n" ;;
+                        sudo) printf "%s\n" "$TEST_FAKE_SUDO" ;;
+                        runuser) return 1 ;;
+                        *) return 1 ;;
+                    esac
+                }
+                exec_as_target_user acfstestuser bash /tmp/acfs-child
+            '
+        assert_success
+        assert_output --partial "HOME=$target_home"
+        assert_output --partial "TARGET_HOME=$target_home"
+        assert_output --partial "ACFS_HOME=$target_home/.acfs"
+        assert_output --partial "ACFS_STATE_FILE=$target_home/.acfs/state.json"
+        refute_output --partial "ACFS_HOME=$stale_home/.acfs"
+        refute_output --partial "ACFS_STATE_FILE=$stale_home/.acfs/state.json"
+        refute_output --partial "ACFS_SYSTEM_STATE_FILE=$stale_home/.acfs/state.json"
+    done
+}

+@test "read-only helper home_for_user functions prefer passwd over stale current home" {
+    local passwd_home
+    local stale_home
+    local label
+    local script
+    local sanitize_func
+    local passwd_func
+    local home_func
+    local current_func
+    local getent_func
+    local current_home_var
+    local expected_output
+    local failures=""
+
+    passwd_home="$(create_temp_dir)"
+    stale_home="$(create_temp_dir)"
+    expected_output="${passwd_home}"$'\n'"${stale_home}"
+
+    while IFS='|' read -r label script sanitize_func passwd_func home_func current_func getent_func current_home_var; do
+        [[ -n "$label" ]] || continue
+
+        run env HOME="$stale_home" PATH="/usr/bin:/bin" bash -s -- \
+            "$script" "$sanitize_func" "$passwd_func" "$home_func" \
+            "$current_func" "$getent_func" "$current_home_var" \
+            "$passwd_home" "$stale_home" <<'EOF'
+set -euo pipefail
+
+script="$1"
+sanitize_func="$2"
+passwd_func="$3"
+home_func="$4"
+current_func="$5"
+getent_func="$6"
+current_home_var="$7"
+passwd_home="$8"
+stale_home="$9"
+
+export ACFS_TEST_CURRENT_USER="tester"
+export ACFS_TEST_PASSWD_HOME="$passwd_home"
+
+eval "$(sed -n "/^${sanitize_func}()/,/^}$/p" "$script")"
+eval "$(sed -n "/^${passwd_func}()/,/^}$/p" "$script")"
+eval "$(sed -n "/^${home_func}()/,/^}$/p" "$script")"
+eval "${current_func}() { printf '%s\n' \"\$ACFS_TEST_CURRENT_USER\"; }"
+eval "${getent_func}() { if [[ \"\${1:-}\" == \"\$ACFS_TEST_CURRENT_USER\" ]]; then printf '%s:x:1000:1000::%s:/bin/bash\n' \"\$ACFS_TEST_CURRENT_USER\" \"\$ACFS_TEST_PASSWD_HOME\"; return 0; fi; return 1; }"
+
+printf -v "$current_home_var" '%s' "$stale_home"
+"$home_func" "$ACFS_TEST_CURRENT_USER"
+
+eval "${getent_func}() { return 1; }"
+"$home_func" "$ACFS_TEST_CURRENT_USER"
+EOF
+
+        if [[ "$status" -ne 0 || "$output" != "$expected_output" ]]; then
+            printf -v failures '%s%s: status=%s output=%s\n' "$failures" "$label" "$status" "$output"
+        fi
+    done <<EOF
+support|$PROJECT_ROOT/scripts/lib/support.sh|support_sanitize_abs_nonroot_path|support_passwd_home_from_entry|support_home_for_user|support_resolve_current_user|support_getent_passwd_entry|_SUPPORT_CURRENT_HOME
+status|$PROJECT_ROOT/scripts/lib/status.sh|_status_sanitize_abs_nonroot_path|_status_passwd_home_from_entry|_status_home_for_user|_status_resolve_current_user|_status_getent_passwd_entry|_STATUS_CURRENT_HOME

+test_fetch_with_backoff_clears_return_trap() {
+    # Regression test: library functions are sourced into long-lived shells, so
+    # temp-file cleanup must not leave a stale RETURN trap behind.
+    local temp_dir tmp_file trap_output status=0
+    temp_dir=$(mktemp -d)
+    tmp_file=$(mktemp)
+
+    cat > "$temp_dir/curl" <<'EOF'
+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+set -euo pipefail
+
+if [[ "${1:-}" == "--help" ]]; then
+    printf '%s\n' '--proto'
+    exit 0
+fi
+
+headers=""
+output=""
+while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
+    case "$1" in
+        -D)
+            headers="$2"
+            shift 2
+            ;;
+        -o)
+            output="$2"
+            shift 2
+            ;;
+        -w)
+            shift 2
+            ;;
+        *)
+            shift
+            ;;
+    esac
+done
+
+printf 'HTTP/2 200\r\nx-ratelimit-remaining: 1\r\n\r\n' > "$headers"
+printf 'ok\n' > "$output"
+printf '200'
+EOF
+    chmod +x "$temp_dir/curl"
+
+    trap_output="$(
+        PATH="$temp_dir:/usr/bin:/bin" GITHUB_MAX_RETRIES=1 bash -c '
+            set -euo pipefail
+            source "$1"
+            github_fetch_with_backoff "https://example.invalid/file" "$2" "trap-test" >/dev/null
+            trap -p RETURN
+        ' _ "$LIB_DIR/github_api.sh" "$tmp_file"
+    )" || status=$?
+
+    rm -rf "$temp_dir"
+    rm -f "$tmp_file"
+
+    [[ "$status" -eq 0 && -z "$trap_output" ]]
+}
+
+test_latest_release_returns_nonzero_on_fetch_failure() {
+    (
+        github_api_fetch() {
+            return 2
+        }
+
+        local output status=0
+        output="$(github_get_latest_release "owner/repo")" || status=$?
+        [[ "$status" -ne 0 && -z "$output" ]]
+    )
+}

+# Test: Custom ACFS_REF is shell-escaped in copy-paste resume hints
+test_custom_ref_shell_escaped() {
+    setup_test_env
+    ACFS_REF_INPUT='bad;touch /tmp/acfs-pwned #'
+
+    local result
+    result=$(generate_resume_hint "" "")
+
+    if [[ "$result" == *"bad;touch"* ]]; then
+        log "  Expected escaped ref in output, got raw shell metacharacters: $result"
+        return 1
+    fi
+
+    if [[ "$result" != *"bad\\;touch"* ]]; then
+        log "  Expected shell-escaped semicolon in output, got: $result"
+        return 1
+    fi
+
+    if [[ "$result" != *"--ref bad\\;touch"* ]]; then
+        log "  Expected --ref value to be escaped, got: $result"
+        return 1
+    fi
+
+    return 0
+}
